title: Glymphatic System
definition: A waste clearance system in the brain that operates during sleep, removing toxic byproducts and supporting cognitive function.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Think of the glymphatic system as your brain's garbage truck, running at night to collect waste and keep your brain clean and healthy.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The glymphatic system is a waste removal network in the brain that functions during sleep, facilitating the clearance of metabolic byproducts and supporting optimal cognitive function.
difficulty_levels.expert: The glymphatic system is a unique cerebrospinal fluid circulation pathway within the brain that facilitates waste clearance, particularly of toxic molecules like beta-amyloid. Its activity during sleep plays a crucial role in preserving cognitive function.

extra_fields.insight: The novel insight is recognizing that sleep serves an essential role in waste clearance from the brain, which has implications for understanding and treating various neurological disorders.
